name: Egyptian Mythology
icon:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 64 64">
  <circle cx="32" cy="32" r="28.8" fill="#9370DB" opacity="0.2"/>
  <circle cx="32" cy="32" r="19.2" fill="none" stroke="#9370DB" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,5"/>
  <text x="32" y="44.8" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22.4" fill="#9370DB">✨</text>
</svg>
description: Egyptian Mythology is a significant ritual practice in egyptian tradition.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the World
keyMyths.description: Atum, arising from the primordial waters of Nun, created the first gods by spitting out Shu (air) and Tefnut (moisture). These deities then gave birth to Geb (earth) and Nut (sky), setting the stage for the world as we know it.

keyMyths.title: The Myth of Osiris
keyMyths.description: Osiris, a benevolent king, was murdered by his jealous brother Set, who scattered his body parts. Isis, Osiris's wife, collected the pieces, resurrected him, and conceived Horus, who would avenge his father's death.

keyMyths.title: The Journey of Ra
keyMyths.description: Each night, the sun god Ra travels through the underworld, battling Apophis, the serpent of chaos, to ensure the sun rises again. This journey symbolizes the constant struggle between order and chaos.

keyMyths.title: The Weighing of the Heart
keyMyths.description: After death, the deceased's heart is weighed against the feather of Ma'at (truth and justice). If the heart is lighter, the soul is worthy to enter the afterlife; if heavier, it is devoured by Ammit.

keyMyths.title: The Contendings of Horus and Set
keyMyths.description: Horus and Set engaged in a lengthy and brutal struggle for the throne of Egypt after Osiris's death. The conflict involved various trials and divine interventions, ultimately leading to Horus's victory and the restoration of order.

sources.name: The Pyramid Texts
sources.description: The oldest known religious texts from ancient Egypt, inscribed on the walls of pyramids, primarily focused on the afterlife and the king's journey.
sources.title: The Pyramid Texts

sources.name: The Coffin Texts
sources.description: A collection of funerary spells and texts inscribed on coffins during the Middle Kingdom, offering guidance and protection to the deceased in the afterlife.
sources.title: The Coffin Texts

sources.name: The Book of the Dead
sources.description: A collection of spells and incantations intended to assist the deceased in navigating the afterlife and achieving immortality, often illustrated on papyrus scrolls.
sources.title: The Book of the Dead

sources.name: Plutarch, *Isis and Osiris*
sources.description: A Greek writer's account of the Egyptian myth of Osiris and Isis, offering insights into Egyptian religious beliefs and practices, though with a Hellenistic interpretation.
sources.title: Plutarch, *Isis and Osiris*

sources.name: Chester Beatty Papyrus I
sources.description: An ancient Egyptian papyrus containing a variety of literary and religious texts, including 'The Contendings of Horus and Set', providing valuable information about Egyptian mythology and beliefs.
sources.title: Chester Beatty Papyrus I
